edges.description: Reduced cerebral perfusion pressure below the autoregulatory threshold produces transient global cerebral hypoperfusion and syncope.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cerebral hypoperfusion below the cerebral autoregulatory threshold

edges.description: Sustained low renal perfusion pressure reduces glomerular filtration and produces ischemic acute kidney injury.
intermediate_mechanisms: Renal hypoperfusion with reduced glomerular filtration

edges.description: Cardiogenic, hypovolemic, obstructive, and distributive shock states reduce cardiac output or systemic vascular tone, producing the abrupt fall in arterial blood pressure that defines acute hypotension.

edges.target: Adrenergic compensation and vasopressor-responsive vascular tone
Adrenergic compensation and vasopressor-responsive vascular tone

nodes.description: Cause-directed hemodynamic support may include fluids, vasopressors, and inotropes to restore tissue perfusion.

evidence.reference_title: The effect of using low dose norepinephrine before hypotensive resuscitation in hemorrhagic shock; a randomized controlled trial
evidence.snippet: Low-dose norepinephrine infusion during the early period of hypotensive resuscitation reduces resuscitative fluid requirement, improves tissue perfusion, preserves renal function, and lowers mortality in hemorrhagic shock patients.

nodes.description: Kidney hypoperfusion is a major morbidity associated with severe or prolonged hypotension.

evidence.reference_title: Postoperative hypotension in patients discharged to the intensive care unit after non-cardiac surgery is associated with adverse clinical outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, we found an association between postoperative hypotension with MAP ≤ 55 mmHg and acute kidney injury stage II/III

nodes.description: Compensatory sympathetic activation and therapeutic catecholamines raise heart rate, vascular tone, and cardiac output to restore perfusion pressure when endogenous compensation is insufficient.
nodes.id: Adrenergic compensation and vasopressor-responsive vascular tone

evidence.reference_title: The effect of using low dose norepinephrine before hypotensive resuscitation in hemorrhagic shock; a randomized controlled trial
evidence.snippet: Group I received a low dose of NE (< 0.3 µg/kg/min) concurrently with resuscitation fluids.

nodes.description: Intramuscular epinephrine is first-line treatment for anaphylaxis, a distributive-shock cause of acute hypotension.

evidence.reference_title: Frequency of cardiotoxicity following intramuscular administration of epinephrine in emergency department patients with anaphylaxis.
evidence.snippet: Among 338 consecutive adult ED patients who received IM epinephrine for anaphylaxis during a recent 4-year period, cardiotoxic side effects were observed in approximately 5% of patients.

nodes.description: Abrupt low blood pressure is the defining physiologic abnormality.

evidence.reference_title: Hypotension during intensive care stay and mortality and morbidity: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: a significant association was found between hypotension and mortality (odds ratio: 1.45; 95% confidence interval (CI) 1.12-1.88

nodes.description: Intravenous isotonic fluid resuscitation is used when acute hypotension is due to hypovolemia or shock with inadequate effective circulating volume.

evidence.reference_title: The effect of using low dose norepinephrine before hypotensive resuscitation in hemorrhagic shock; a randomized controlled trial
evidence.snippet: Group II received resuscitative fluids only. If the resuscitative fluids failed to keep mean arterial pressure (MAP) > 65 mmHg, the patient was progressively administered NE even with high doses (0.05 to more than 0.3 µg/kg/min).

nodes.description: Ongoing monitoring of blood pressure and organ injury markers is important because postoperative and ICU hypotension is associated with mortality, major adverse cardiovascular or cerebrovascular events, and AKI.

evidence.reference_title: Postoperative hypotension in patients discharged to the intensive care unit after non-cardiac surgery is associated with adverse clinical outcomes.
evidence.snippet: Postoperative hypotension in critical care patients with MAP ≤ 65 mmHg is associated with adverse events even without experiencing intraoperative hypotension.

nodes.description: Acute hypotension lowers perfusion pressure and can reduce oxygen delivery to vulnerable organs, especially when mean arterial pressure reductions are severe or prolonged.

evidence.reference_title: Hypotension during intensive care stay and mortality and morbidity: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: Exposure to hypotension during ICU stay was associated with increased mortality and AKI in the majority of included studies, and associations for both outcomes increased with increasing hypotension severity.

nodes.description: Acute hypotension can occur as part of cardiogenic, hypovolemic, obstructive, or distributive shock, converging on inadequate tissue oxygen delivery and organ dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: The effect of using low dose norepinephrine before hypotensive resuscitation in hemorrhagic shock; a randomized controlled trial
evidence.snippet: This randomized controlled trial was conducted on 200 participants, at least 18 years old, classified as severely traumatized and had significant hemorrhage with mean arterial pressure ranging from 65 to 75 mmHg.

nodes.description: Cerebral hypoperfusion from acute hypotension can produce transient loss of consciousness.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological aspects of syncope and orthostatic intolerance.
evidence.snippet: Sudden falling with loss of consciousness from syncope and symptoms of orthostatic intolerance are common, dramatic clinical problems of diverse cause, but cerebral hypoperfusion is the ultimate mechanism in most.
